metadata.description: Pre-submission v8 audit data: ICA pipeline, BDI subgroup stats, ANCOVA scar test

task_1a_ica_pipeline.n_components: variable per subject: n_channels - n_bad_channels_FASTER (60-channel cap minus FASTER-flagged bad channels)

ocular_components: VEOG correlation |z| > 3 across all ICA components
artifact_rejection_criteria.note_ADJUST: ADJUST algorithm was disabled (commented out) in the preprocessing script

channel_rejection.FASTER_criteria: z > 3 on: weighted correlation, variance, Hurst exponent

epoch_rejection.criteria: z > 3 on any of: mean epoch deviation, epoch variance, max amplitude

task_1a_ica_pipeline.reference: linked mastoids (M1 + M2), applied after channel interpolation
task_1a_ica_pipeline.note: Data arrive pre-epoched in .mat format (EEGLAB); our MNE pipeline (01_preprocessing_cavanagh.py) only converts format — no additional preprocessing applied

task_2_bdi_subgroups.note: BDI=Beck Depression Inventory; HamD=Hamilton Depression Rating Scale

ancova_group_and_HamD.HamD_interpretation: non-significant — severity does not explain the scar effect

interaction_test.interpretation: non-significant — scar effect not moderated by symptom severity

MDD_current_vs_MDD_past_HamD_adjusted.note: HamD residualized within MDD only (CTL have no HamD)

task_3_ancova_scar.interpretation: Scar effect confirmed: MDD_past has lower AIS_pre than MDD_current despite lower HamD. CTL vs MDD_past d=1.175 > CTL vs MDD_current d=0.389, showing the neural deficit is LARGER in remitted depression. Severity (HamD) is non-significant in ANCOVA and the interaction is null, ruling out symptom confound.
